title: Scone Cup and Doomben 10,000 Racing Special with Jimmy Cassidy.
summary: Get ready to kick off Season 4 of "Who's the Boss" with a galloping adventure in horse racing! Join Adam Peacock and legendary jockey Glenn Boss as they step out of the studio to explore the breathtaking Arrowfield Stud. With thousands of acres dedicated to the finest breeding, this iconic farm sets the stage for thrilling discussions and fresh air escapades. Feel the excitement as they delve into the serene world of equine champions, building anticipation for an in-depth chat with the master breeder himself, John Messara, next week. The action heats up as we trot through Australia's vibrant racing scene—Scone's country races and Brisbane's winter carnival await! Tune in as Jim Cassidy, aka "Pumper," shares his predictions for the Scone Races and dish out insider scoops on favourites and long shots alike. Relive the romance of country carnivals, where spirit and racing heritage combine, and get ready to cheer as Bossy and Pumper recount their finest triumphs, from the legendary Rough Habit to their thrilling private aviation tales. Rounding off with a barn-busting look at upcoming major circuits, explore the Winter Carnival’s top contenders and the unpredictable elements that shape the races. With stories of beloved horses and choice breeding tales, this episode is a race to the finish, complete with laughter, camaraderie, and pure racing passion. Don't forget to hit that like button and subscribe for more adrenaline-fueled episodes right here! Time Stamps [00:00:08] - Season 4 Begins: Out of the Studio Adventure Kicking off the new season from the breathtaking Arrowfield Stud. [00:02:10] - The Heart of Horse Racing: Arrowfield Stud Tour Explore the history and significance of one of the world’s premier breeding complexes. [00:01:48] - Behind the Scenes: Trophy Rooms and Memories Dive into a world of trophies and racing achievements with Jim Cassidy. [00:06:10] - Country Carnivals: The Charm of Scone Races Feel the unique vibe and embrace the community spirit of country racing. [00:08:47] - Racing Legends: Stories from the Track Hear legendary tales of unexpected rides and memorable wins in bush races. [00:14:46] - Queensland Racing: Brisbane Winter Carnival Kicks Off Discover the strategic importance of winter races for future champions. [00:16:46] - Rough Habit Legacy: A Horse Like No Other Relive the iconic moments and achievements of the legendary Rough Habit. [00:25:40] - Bossy's Best: Upcoming Races and Betting Insights Get insider tips and analyses for the Scone Cup and Doomben 10,000. [00:35:47] - Sydney’s Racing Future: Controversial Developments Discuss potential changes to racing venues and their impact on the industry. title: UNCLE PAUL BONIFAY EPISODE 3
summary: UNCLE PAUL BONIFAY AKA "THE BLACKSHEEP BONIFAY" In this episode of Banter Banter, Parks and Shane sit down with their legendary Uncle Paul—and nothing’s off the table. From rushing the stage at Parks’ documentary premiere to their dad’s motorcycle wreck and barefooting at 104 mph, the stories run deep. The texture of Uncle Paul’s voice adds a grit and authenticity you can feel. Hearing a Bonifay speak so openly about life, family, and wild adventures gives this episode a raw, personal edge you won’t find anywhere else. It’s an unfiltered front-row seat into what it truly means to be a Bonifay. And as a bonus? You’ll get a teaser of Black Bonifay—an original song Parks wrote about the elusive Uncle Paul himself. Don’t miss it. -The Bonifay Banter

title: #400: Luke Humphries, Barry Hearn, Dave Allen, ProTour Review, Mailbag
summary: Alex Moss and Burton DeWitt are back with a 400th episode special!  PDC world number one Luke Humphries (1:19) sits down with Alex during last week's Premier League media day in Leeds to reflect on the last 18 months since becoming world champion. Luke looks back at his PDC World Championship final win against Luke Littler, adding to his major tally with more landmark wins, including the World Matchplay and World Masters, as well as discussing how he has coped with the fame and his role as an ambassador for the game.  The boys start this week's milestone editions of the podcast by looking back on the big stories that have happened during the last 100 episodes. Alex and Burton also pick out some of their favourite guests on the show from the first 400 episodes.  PDC president Barry Hearn (37:22) joins Burton for a wide-ranging chat on all things darts. Barry delves into the PDC's recent prize money increase announcement, the organisation's new five-year deal with Sky Sports, as well as his recent conversation with Turki Al-Sheikh about bringing darts to Saudi Arabia. Barry also outlines his future plans for North America and his hopes to add a number of events to the calendar in the US and Canada in 2026.  Alex and Burton then give their review of the ProTour action in Germany this week, which saw Krzysztof Ratajski and Ross Smith both open their title accounts for the season.  PDC head of media Dave Allen (1:18:27) chats with Alex at last week's Premier League media day about his 20+ years working for the darts leader. Dave discusses how his move to join the PDC's media team first came about in 2004, and how the media landscape has changed over the last two decades, including the introduction of social media and the increase in media interest. Dave also picks out some of his most memorable moments working for the PDC and his favourite places visited during his time covering events on the tour.  The boys finish off the 400th episode special with a dip into the mailbag to answer your listener questions. title: Ep 40 1995 State of Origin Series | Part 2
summary: In this second and final instalment of our 1995 Origin 30-Year Anniversary Special, Trevor Gillmeister, Billy Moore, and Ben Ikin return to take us inside one of the most courageous and emotional victories in Maroons history — the Game III triumph at Lang Park. With the series already secured, Queensland entered Game III battered, bruised, and still written off by the experts. What followed was a gritty, defiant performance that completed a remarkable 3–0 clean sweep and cemented this team’s place in rugby league folklore. In this episode, the boys reflect on: Gillmeister’s legendary effort to play after checking himself out of hospitalThe brutal exchanges and explosive all-in brawlsBen Ikin’s breakout moment and first Origin try at just 18 years oldJason Smith’s masterclass, Bartrim’s consistency, and Lam’s brillianceThe brotherhood, belief, and emotion that powered the ‘95 MaroonsThis was more than just a series win — it was a cultural moment that reshaped Queensland’s Origin identity. #FOGS #QLDER #FOGSQueenslander #Podcast #1995Origin #Game3 #TrevorGillmeister #BillyMoore #BenIkin #QueenslandMaroons #MarkBraybrook #StateOfOrigin #NevilleNobodies #OriginLegends

title: Henry de Bromhead’s Tribute to Rachael Blackmore & Basher Watts Sounds Off on French 1000 Guineas Fallout
summary: In this Final Furlong audio podcast exclusive, we reflect on two major moments that have rocked the racing world. First, Rachael Blackmore — the groundbreaking, history-making champion — has announced her retirement. From a 7lb claimer at Clonmel to winning every major championship race at Cheltenham and rewriting the Grand National record books aboard Minella Times, Rachael inspired a generation. Emmet Kennedy is joined by the man behind so many of those iconic victories, Henry de Bromhead, to pay tribute to a jockey who changed the sport forever. Then, we turn to Longchamp, where controversy erupted in the aftermath of the French 1,000 Guineas. She’s Perfect crossed the line first — seemingly handing a first Classic win to trainer Charlie Fellowes and owner Basher Watts — only to be demoted after a stewards’ inquiry in favour of a whipless Zarigana. What happened, and what does it say about racing’s rules? Basher Watts joins us to give his first full reaction — and it’s unmissable. title: Huge Man United double-header with UCL football & FA Cup glory on the line
podcast_name: Straight Outta Cobham: The Athletic FC's Chelsea show
summary: Surely they have to beat *this* Man United team?! Matt Davies-Adams, Liam Twomey and Sam Parkin assemble to look ahead to a huge Friday night and Sunday afternoon for Chelsea Football Club. We begin under the lights at Stamford Bridge, where at 20:15 on Friday evening the Blues host a Manchester United side who sit 16th in the table, haven't won a game in the Premier League since the 16th March, haven’t won back-to-back league games all season AND are  averaging less than a point a game under manager Ruben Amorim.  So - why are we still not 100% sure that Enzo Maresca's side will be able to come away with the win? And who's going to fill the void left behind by Nicholas Jackson? The Athletic's United writer Carl Anka is on hand to give us the inside scoop on the opponents and their struggles.  Then it's all about CFCW - looking to wrap up the domestic treble on Sunday afternoon when they take on Manchester United at Wembley. Carl sticks around to help us preview that one too as Sonia Bompastor's side look to sign off from this season in style.  And - of course - we finish with a fiendishly tricky quiz... We'll be back on Monday to run the rule over the Manchester United double header in the Premier League and Women's FA Cup Final!  HOST: Matt Davies-Adams  WITH: Liam Twomey, Sam Parkin and Carl Anka PRODUCER: Lucy Oliva  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

summary: Welcome to this special two-part Talk of the Devils Extra, a detailed history of the controversial purchase and subsequent ownership of Manchester United by American investor Malcolm Glazer and his six children. This first part will recount the events leading up to the Glazer takeover in 2005 with contributions from three key perspectives; fan activist Andy Walsh, former Labour Minister for Sport Richard Caborn, and United board member, economist Jim O'Neill. In part two to follow 24 hours later we will bring the story up to the present day, analyse the relationship between the Glazers and minority owner Sir Jim Ratcliffe, and try to forecast the future of United's ownership. title: Why The Premier League NEEDS Relegation Playoffs…
summary: On this week’s episode of That Peter Crouch Podcast, Crouchy, Chris, and Sids kick back with a glass of cognac as they dive into some of your listener messages. From awkward handshakes and cultural greeting rituals to the ultimate pub chat: could a relegation playoff transform the Premier League? The lads unpack a genius listener suggestion for turning end-of-season misery into box office gold. They also spotlight a football league where weight loss adds goals, discuss the tragicomic reality of football freestyle stars not making it pro, and weigh in on Arsenal’s growing rep for “Spursiness.” There’s a heartfelt moment too, as a listener shares how Chris’s cancer story helped during his own diagnosis—reminding everyone to check their balls. With a powerful mix of humour, nostalgia, and top-tier pub debate, this is a classic “messages” episode—and maybe the start of a Premier League revolution. In this NW200 exclusive, our host Dave Neal sits down with a great lineup of road racing talent and media personalities including: 👉 Jamie Coward – Top TT & road racer, sharing his thoughts on the NW200 and season ambitions  👉 Chris Anderson – Crew Chief to Ian Hutchinson  👉 Mitch Rees – New Zealand's superbike ace on Road Racing  👉 Chris Pritchard – Cycling legend turned motorsport media star gatecrashed by Steve Parrish and Keith Huewen  👉 Paul Jordan – Magherafelt’s road racing hero, fresh from the grid In this candid and fast-paced conversation, we dive into everything from the thrill of the Triangle Circuit, to rider mindset, team strategies, and what makes the North West 200 such a unique road racing spectacle. 🔧 Get behind-the-scenes paddock stories  🎙️ Hear exclusive rider interviews  📈 Learn what it takes to race at the NW200 level Whether you're a TT fan, Irish road racing enthusiast, or just love the raw adrenaline of real motorcycle racing, this episode delivers the goods. 📍 Recorded in the North West 200 paddock, May 2025 Send us a text  Support the show Would you like early access to shows and the chance to ask questions of the guests? title: Murray Mexted: Blues & Hurricanes are 'Sleeping Giants' in Super Rugby, All Blacks vs France, & NZ Rugby's BIG Financial Loss
summary: All Blacks legend Murray Mexted joins Martin Devlin on the DSPN, talking a whole lot of rugby with the Super Rugby Pacific finals fast-approaching. Marty and Mex list their top contenders, while referencing incredible recent results such as the Chiefs beating the Crusaders on the road, and the Hurricanes upsetting the Waikato-based franchise in the capital. Are the Blues flying under the radar? Mex reckons so. The ex All Blacks loosie also chats about the upcoming French tour of New Zealand and the threat Les Bleus will pose, his experiences playing in France, New Zealand Rugby's recent $19.5 million financial loss, and the need to prioritise grassroots rugby.     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: Gabby Logan: Midlife, Mics and Making Her Mark.
podcast_name: A LOAD OF BS ON SPORT
summary: Gabby Logan joins us for a brilliant conversation that spans her journey from elite gymnast to one of the most recognisable faces in British sports broadcasting. From her early days growing up in a sporting family to breaking through in a male-dominated industry, Gabby shares the lessons, laughs, and life skills she’s picked up along the way. We talk about resilience, moving schools, and why losing is just as important as winning. Gabby reflects on the power of participation in sport—for confidence, wellbeing, and those all-important life lessons. She opens up about the unique challenges of live broadcasting, the importance of empathy in journalism, and how women in sports media are paving the way for the next generation. Plus, we get Gabby’s take on midlife as a moment of power, why podcasting is her new favourite medium, and whether she’d ever host a mastermind-style sports quiz show. It’s candid, inspiring, and full of warmth—just like Gabby herself. What You’ll Hear - Gabby’s journey from gymnastics to the BBC and beyond - How her sporting family shaped her career and confidence - The role of resilience, especially when moving schools as a child - Why participation in sport matters for everyone—not just the winners - The art of natural, empathetic broadcasting (and a few Alan Partridge moments!) - Navigating and thriving in a male-dominated industry - How pivotal life experiences inform our values and choices - Embracing midlife as a time of new opportunities, not decline - The joys of podcasting and community - Thoughts on health, fitness, and redefining success in your 50s - Why quizzes and a competitive spirit can be great for personal growth Memorable Quotes “I want people at home to feel they are there, but equally there’s a little bit of them wanting to be inside that stadium.” “Participation in sports teaches valuable life lessons about winning and losing.” “Empathy is essential in sports broadcasting.” “Women in sports broadcasting are paving the way for future generations.” If you enjoyed this episode, please subscribe and leave a review! #GabbyLogan #SportsBroadcasting #WomenInSport #PersonalDevelopment #Resilience #MidlifePower #Podcasting #Gymnastics #Broadcasting #SportConfidence  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: V8SP: Cameron McLean (Part 2)
summary: This week’s edition of the V8 Sleuth Podcast powered by Autobarn features popular V8 Supercars and Super Touring privateer Cameron McLean. The Queenslander was a fixture of touring car racing grids in the 1990s and early 2000s in the yellow hues of Greenfield Mowers, initially in two-litre racing in a variety of ex-factory machines before making the leap to V8s with Ford Falcons. Among the second and final part of our two-part chat with Cam are his years of being a Supercars team co-owner, including his side of the story of his team’s car being seized and impounded in the middle of a race weekend. He also shares a sliding-doors moment that nearly led him to a full-time factory V8 Supercars drive, his time as a hired gun for the V8 enduros, and his scary roll in Carrera Cup at Queensland Raceway. title: Beers (and Chelsea sandwich) for Breakfast

summary: Eddie Howe's trophy-winning Mags are on the brink of returning to the Champions League next season after a lightning start and a thunderous finish provided emphatic bookends to a nervy bit in between. The final result was 2-0, at least according to most reliable judges, and with two games to go, it's difficult to see how Newcastle can lose three places in the league. In fact with a trip to Arsenal next, might we be looking up instead! Helping us savour the success this week is special guest Paul Ferris, NUFC's favourite polymath, who has a new book out but doesn't need any help selling tickets to the launch. title: 264: Max Rushden (Pt 1)
summary: Elis James (Elis & John, Oh What A Time), Mike Bubbins (Mammoth), and Steff Garrero return with another round of top-tier sporting nonsense, barely researched takes, and an absolutely brilliant special guest. “Like a sports podcast, if the sport was optional.” ✨ This Week’s Special Guest – Max Rushden! If you’ve enjoyed our guest episodes with Chesney Hawkes, Josh Widdicombe, or Sam Warburton, you’re in for a treat. This week we welcome Max Rushden — broadcaster, podcaster, football obsessive, and all-round good guy. Max is the long-running host of Guardian Football Weekly, where he teams up with our friend Barry Glendenning for football chat, laughs, and chaos.  He’s also recently launched a new podcast with comedian David O’Doherty called What Did You Do Yesterday?, where they interview fascinating guests about a completely ordinary day. 🎧 Guardian Football Weekly  🎧 What Did You Do Yesterday? Max’s broadcasting career has spanned BBC Radio 5 Live, talkSPORT, Channel 4’s Jump Off, and iconic years hosting Sky’s Soccer AM.  Known for his wit, love of the underdog, and eye for the ridiculous, Max fits right in at the Distant Pod. 🎟️ We’re Going on Tour in 2026! We’re hitting the road with a brand-new live show in 2026!  Tickets on general sale now – head to distantpod.com/live for all the details. 📍 Tour Dates: Fri 20 March 2026 – Cardiff, New TheatreSat 21 March 2026 – Cardiff, New TheatreTue 7 April 2026 – London, Hackney EmpireWed 8 April 2026 – London, Hackney EmpireFri 24 April 2026 – Carmarthen, Lyric Theatre🎉 This Week’s Show – Don’t Miss It! Max has brought some absolute gems to the show: 🥫 Baked Beans, Penalties – Max and Charlie Baker  🔗 Watch on X/Twitter 🐎 Man Versus Beast  Yes, humans really raced animals on TV in 2003. No, we can’t quite believe it either.  🔗 Watch on YouTube ⛸️ Steven Bradbury – Australian Speed Skating Gold, 2002  The greatest underdog gold medal of all time.  🔗 Watch on YouTube  (Yes, Joe Marler wishes he picked it, but no, it wasn’t actually his choice. And yes, technically Steff picked it for a live stream, but… we’ve lost the tapes.) 📖 A Little Extra Gift from Max Max also shared this brilliant nostalgic piece that perfectly fits the vibe of the pod:  🔗 Vonk, Zoeller, Tomba… The Joy of Sporting Names (The Guardian) 💡 Get More of the Pod Every Week 🎧 Patreon gets you: Bonus episodes & extra clipsMichael Owen’s Movie Club & Hoffi PodEarly access & live tour presaleGift memberships🚀 Prefer Wondery+? title: Aidan Walsh. Being let go. Winning an All-Ireland, moving to Kerry
summary: Life after inter-county GAA takes a period of time to adjust. When your a Cork hurler and footballer you've dined in two hotels- both five star. Life has now brought Aidan Walsh to West Kerry. He transferred to the home of the O'Se's An Ghaeltacht and life is good. Love can do that sort of thing. A beautiful wee boy (yes-wearing a Kerry jersey) has changed perspective. Living in one of the most beautiful places in Ireland- but he can't speak the language. In this podcast we discuss being dropped at 30, why Aidan struggles to go and watch Cork Hurlers but believes (and hopes) they will win the All-Ireland this year. He also makes Patrick Horgan's hurleys- his is a bit different. It's a great catch-up with an All-Ireland winning footballer, a gifted hurler and a man who is at home in Kerry-but always Cork.

title: Moto PG 162: All The Different Flavoured Lubes
summary: When you put the word 'French' in front of other words (like 'sex' or 'kissing' or 'fries') magic happens. But when. you put it in front of the sentence 'victory in their home Moto GP for the first time in history' things go to a whole other level. Johann Zarco became an instant national hero when he made history as the first French rider to win the premiere class at Le Mans and the team at The Greatest Motorcycling Podcast in the Universe are here for all of it. Boris, Tug and Freido are joined by special in studio guest Pete Vorst of Compass Expeditions as they break down one of the most memorable race wins in years. There's also Boris' poem (an ode to Zarco), Freido's horoscope (also about Zarco...there's a pattern emerging here) and the 'Simone Flatterson Man Boobs and Grid Girl Critique' (which isn't about Zarco but is still magnificent) and the rest of the puerile nonsense you've come to expect from these three reprobates. Hit the play button and enjoy the gloriousness of it. It will make you laugh, it will make you cry and it it will make you irresistably attractive to the opposite sex. You've been warned.

title: Let the dominoes fall after UFC 315
summary: The CME’s 13th birthday is coming up, and you know what that means: It’s pledge month! Now through June 1, GET 50% OFF A NEW MONTHLY OR ANNUAL PATREON SUBSCRIPTION when you use the code CME13 at checkout! Get on it! Join the team! Support the community! Stick it to the man! Did Jack Della Maddalena ruin everything? Well, it’s a fluid situation. As we recorded this episode of the Proper, the MMA world awaited an announcement from Dana White we all assume(d) will be the main event of UFC 317 coming up in June for International Fight Week. What will we get? Ilia Topuria vs. Islam Makhachev? Or Topuria vs. Charles Oliveira? By the time you listen to this, you might well know the answer. It’s possible that Della Maddalena’s welterweight title win over Belal Muhammad at last weekend’s UFC 315 will tip a succession of dominoes that could result in Makhachev moving up to 170 pounds to fight JDM, which would leave Topuria to fight Oliveira.  And let's be honest, that would kinda suck. It was a brilliant performance by Flatnose Jackie to take the title on Saturday. But at what cost? Learn more about your ad choices. title: The Salt Lake City Review Show
summary: That is a wrap on one incredible 2025 SX season! Salt Lake brought drama with the 250 East class title fight being separated between 3 riders by just 3 points, only leading to fireworks as a latch ditch efforts started to fly during the main event. The 450 class looked to be locked up for the most part, but Chase still rode incredibly strong showing he is the FASTEST rider in the class, but listen in as Justin and Adam break down their thoughts on who really is the "best" rider in each class and what goes into that.  Thanks for submitting your questions this week! And as always thank you all for the support and tuning in to another episode! title: BEYOND BOXING EP260 - BOXING’S LOST GENERATION
summary: Did the pandemic of 2020 do more to hurt boxing than simply deprive boxing fans of fights that they wanted to see? As time goes on it’s clear that there’s a lost generation within Boxing a group of amateurs who missed 2.5 years of vital development that would’ve helped their career immeasurably. As a result what we’re seeing amongst this generation is many of these boxes trying to play catch-up without being given the opportunities to catch up.
